name: Arthur Schutt
abstract: Arthur Schutt (November 21, 1902 – January 28, 1965) was an American jazz pianist and arranger. Schutt was born in Reading, Pennsylvania, United States, and learned piano from his father.

concerts.label: Benefit: American Committee for Relief of German Children

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: it was he and I and Arthur Schutt and Jimmy Dorsey playing clarinet

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Wiggs
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWEE: used to come to rehearsals with a high hat on you remember that fella played with Red Nichols Bunch

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: He was the guy I attached myself to. ... Arthur Schutt and Manny Klein. He knew everybody.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
